title: AUC-Optimized OOF Stacking With Sigmoid-Only Calibration
summary: Because the metric is ROC AUC, the final blend should optimize ranking directly.
rationale: Because the metric is ROC AUC, the final blend should optimize ranking directly. AUC-maximizing metalearners are designed for this, while scikit-learn notes isotonic calibration can change AUC by introducing ties; sigmoid calibration preserves ranking.
implementation_hint: Collect OOF logits/probabilities from diverse specialists, optionally sigmoid-calibrate each model fold-locally, then fit a simple AUC-oriented meta-learner on OOF predictions such as pairwise/logit rank optimization or constrained linear blending tuned on AUC. Avoid isotonic unless it clearly helps despite tie risk.
expected_effect: Often yields small but real leaderboard lift by exploiting complementary ranking errors without degrading AUC through tie-heavy calibration.
risk: Overfitting is easy when the base models are highly correlated; enforce nonnegative weights or strong regularization and validate on race-grouped folds only.
